title: One-Step Bayes: The Biased Coin

runtime.expected_outcome: After one observation the agent's belief mass concentrates near the tile whose wall signature matches the obs; posterior is recognisably non-uniform.

math.latex: P(s \mid o) = \frac{P(o \mid s) \, P(s)}{\sum_{s'} P(o \mid s') \, P(s')}

symbols.s: hidden state (which tile the agent is on)
symbols.P(s): prior over states (uniform before first obs)

audiences.kid: You've just peeked at a clue and now you can narrow down which room you're in. Bayes is the arithmetic that tells you how much to shift your guess.
audiences.real: Bayesian update in one step: prior times likelihood, divided by the total. The agent observes a wall-signature and reweights tiles accordingly.
audiences.equation: P(s|o) \propto P(o|s) P(s). Concretely, take the column of A indexed by the observed o; multiply by the prior D; renormalise.
audiences.derivation: Starting from the joint P(o,s) = P(o|s)P(s) and the sum rule for the marginal P(o) = sum_s P(o|s)P(s), the posterior follows by Bayes' theorem. In the POMDP bundle this reduces to a single softmax over log-likelihoods plus log-prior.

orchestrate.objective: Show a single Bayes update in the POMDP bundle's perceive action, producing a recognisably non-uniform posterior over tiles.
orchestrate.role: Concept tutor and sanity-check for the simplest belief update.
orchestrate.context: A tiny 3x3 maze where the agent has a uniform prior and updates it once on the wall-signature observation at the start tile.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 2.
